name: Root
description: Ledger — Schema Registry and Data Obligation Manager. Composition root and public API facade for the distributed stack (Pact, Arbiter, Baton, Sentinel, Constrain). Defines shared cross-cutting types (enums, violation models, error hierarchy) in a leaf types module with zero internal imports, structural Protocol interfaces for each subsystem, and a bootstrap factory that loads config, constructs all subsystems, and returns a composed frozen Ledger application container. The root __init__.py re-exports __version__, shared types, protocols, and the create_ledger() factory with an explicit __all__. No business logic lives in this module — it is purely a composition and re-export layer. Import direction is strictly enforced: types.py and protocols.py import nothing from ledger.* subpackages; subpackages import only from ledger.types and ledger.protocols; subpackages never import from sibling subpackages at module level; root __init__.py imports downward only into subpackages.

types.name: Severity
types.description: Canonical severity levels for violations, gate results, and validation findings. Shared across all subpackages via ledger.types. Implemented as StrEnum in Python.

types.name: BackendType
types.description: Supported backend storage types in the Ledger ecosystem. Shared canonical enum re-exported from ledger.types.

types.name: ExportFormat
types.description: Target contract formats for the export subsystem. Each variant names a downstream tool that consumes Ledger exports.

types.name: PlanStatus
types.description: Lifecycle status of a migration plan. Transitions are strictly PENDING -> APPROVED or PENDING -> REJECTED; terminal states cannot transition further.

types.name: ClassificationTier
types.description: Fixed data classification tiers. Exactly five members; no runtime additions or removals permitted. Used to classify fields for governance, masking, and gate decisions.

types.name: Violation

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity classification of this violation.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description of the violation.

fields.name: code
fields.description: Machine-readable violation code, e.g. 'MISSING_PK', 'PII_NO_OBLIGATION', 'ANNOTATION_CONFLICT'.

fields.name: path
fields.description: Dot-delimited path to the offending field or entity, e.g. 'backend.users.columns.email'. Empty for global violations.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Additional structured key-value context for programmatic consumers. Keys and values are strings.

types.description: Canonical violation model shared across all subpackages. Carries structured context for a single validation finding, gate result, or export issue. All validation functions collect ALL violations rather than short-circuiting on the first.

types.name: LedgerError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Top-level human-readable error summary.

fields.name: violations
fields.description: Complete list of all violations. Never truncated to just the first.

validators.error_message: LedgerError must carry at least one violation.

fields.name: exit_code
fields.description: Process exit code: 0=success, 1=domain error, 2=usage error, 3=config error, 130=keyboard interrupt.

types.description: Base error type for all Ledger domain errors. Carries one or more structured Violation instances plus an exit code for CLI surfacing. All subpackage-specific errors inherit from this. Raised by domain modules, caught by CLI and API error handlers.

types.name: ViolationList
types.description: A list of Violation instances. Used throughout the system to aggregate all findings.

types.name: RegistryProtocol

fields.name: init
fields.description: Callable[[Path], None] — initializes .ledger/ directory.

fields.name: register_backend
fields.description: Callable[[Path, BackendMetadata, str], ChangelogEntry] — registers a backend.

fields.name: store_schema
fields.description: Callable[[Path, str, str, bytes, str], ChangelogEntry] — stores a schema YAML verbatim.

fields.name: list_backends
fields.description: Callable[[Path], list[BackendMetadata]] — lists all registered backends.

fields.name: list_schemas
fields.description: Callable[[Path, str], list[SchemaRecord]] — lists schemas for a backend.

fields.name: get_schema
fields.description: Callable[[Path, str, str], SchemaRecord | None] — gets a single schema.

fields.name: validate_all
fields.description: Callable[[Path], ValidationResult] — validates all schemas, returns ALL violations.

fields.name: read_changelog
fields.description: Callable[[Path, str | None, int], list[ChangelogEntry]] — reads changelog entries.

types.description: Structural typing.Protocol defining the interface the CLI and API expect from the registry subsystem. Concrete implementations in the registry subpackage satisfy this structurally. Methods: init(root) -> None, register_backend(root, metadata, actor) -> any, store_schema(root, backend_id, table, raw_yaml, actor) -> any, list_backends(root) -> list, list_schemas(root, backend_id) -> list, get_schema(root, backend_id, table) -> any, validate_all(root) -> any, read_changelog(root, backend_id, limit) -> list.

types.name: MigrationProtocol

fields.name: parse_migration
fields.description: Callable[[str, str], ParsedMigration] — parses SQL migration file.

fields.name: compute_diff
fields.description: Callable[[ParsedMigration, any], SchemaDiff] — computes enriched diff.

fields.name: evaluate_gates
fields.description: Callable[[SchemaDiff, ComponentContext], list[GateViolation]] — runs gate checks.

fields.name: create_plan
fields.description: Callable[[SchemaDiff, list, any, str], MigrationPlan] — creates and persists a plan.

fields.name: approve_plan
fields.description: Callable[[str, str, str, str, str], MigrationPlan] — approves a pending plan.

fields.name: load_plan
fields.description: Callable[[str, str], MigrationPlan] — loads a persisted plan.

types.description: Structural typing.Protocol defining the interface for the migration subsystem. Methods: parse_migration(sql, source_path) -> any, compute_diff(parsed, registry) -> any, evaluate_gates(diff, component_context) -> list, create_plan(diff, violations, registry, plans_dir) -> any, approve_plan(plan_id, reviewer, review_ref, rationale, plans_dir) -> any, load_plan(plan_id, plans_dir) -> any.

types.name: ExportProtocol

fields.name: export_pact
fields.description: Callable[[str, list], ExportResultPact] — generates Pact contract assertions.

fields.name: export_arbiter
fields.description: Callable[[list, str], ExportResultArbiter] — generates Arbiter classification rules.

fields.name: export_baton
fields.description: Callable[[list], ExportResultBaton] — generates Baton egress nodes.

fields.name: export_sentinel
fields.description: Callable[[list], ExportResultSentinel] — generates Sentinel severity mappings.

fields.name: yaml_dump
fields.description: Callable[[any], str] — serializes export model to deterministic YAML.

types.description: Structural typing.Protocol defining the interface for the export subsystem. Methods: export_pact(component_id, propagation_table) -> any, export_arbiter(propagation_table, default_backend) -> any, export_baton(propagation_table) -> any, export_sentinel(propagation_table) -> any, yaml_dump(export_model) -> str.

types.name: MockProtocol

fields.name: generate_mock_records
fields.description: Callable[[MockGenerationRequest], MockGenerationResult] — generates mock data records.

fields.name: resolve_seed
fields.description: Callable[[int | None, int | None], int] — resolves the seed for deterministic generation.

types.description: Structural typing.Protocol defining the interface for the mock data generator subsystem. Methods: generate_mock_records(request) -> any, resolve_seed(explicit, config) -> int.

types.name: ConfigProtocol

fields.name: load_config
fields.description: Callable[[str], LedgerConfig] — loads and validates ledger.yaml.

fields.name: build_propagation_table
fields.description: Callable[[list], dict] — merges custom annotations with builtins.

fields.name: validate_annotation_set
fields.description: Callable[[list[str]], list[ConstraintViolation]] — validates annotation set against CONFLICTS/REQUIRES.

fields.name: get_builtin_propagation_table
fields.description: Callable[[], dict] — returns immutable builtin propagation table.

fields.name: get_conflicts
fields.description: Callable[[], frozenset] — returns CONFLICTS constant.

fields.name: get_requires
fields.description: Callable[[], dict] — returns REQUIRES constant.

fields.name: parse_schema_file
fields.description: Callable[[str, dict], SchemaFile] — parses a single schema YAML file.

types.description: Structural typing.Protocol defining the interface for the config subsystem. Methods: load_config(path) -> LedgerConfig, build_propagation_table(custom_annotations) -> dict, validate_annotation_set(annotations) -> list, get_builtin_propagation_table() -> dict, get_conflicts() -> any, get_requires() -> dict, parse_schema_file(path, propagation_table) -> SchemaFile.

types.name: ApiProtocol

fields.name: create_app
fields.description: Callable[[LedgerConfig], FastAPI] — application factory for the HTTP server.

fields.name: serve_cli
fields.description: Callable[[int, str, str], None] — starts uvicorn server, blocks until terminated.

types.description: Structural typing.Protocol defining the interface for the HTTP API subsystem. Methods: create_app(config) -> any, serve_cli(port, host, config_path) -> None.

types.name: Ledger

fields.name: version
fields.description: Ledger version string sourced from pyproject.toml via importlib.metadata.version('ledger'). Falls back to '0.0.0-dev' if not installed.

fields.name: config
fields.description: Loaded and validated LedgerConfig instance from the config subsystem.

fields.name: registry
fields.description: Registry subsystem instance satisfying RegistryProtocol.

fields.name: migration
fields.description: Migration subsystem module/instance satisfying MigrationProtocol.

fields.name: export
fields.description: Export subsystem module/instance satisfying ExportProtocol.

fields.name: mock
fields.description: Mock data generator subsystem module/instance satisfying MockProtocol.

fields.name: api
fields.description: API subsystem module/instance satisfying ApiProtocol.

types.description: Frozen composed application container returned by create_ledger(). Holds references to all subsystem instances and the loaded config. This is the primary object consumers interact with after bootstrap. Satisfies all protocol interfaces by delegation to contained subsystem instances.

types.name: BootstrapError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable summary of the bootstrap failure.

fields.name: violations
fields.description: All violations encountered during bootstrap (config parse errors, missing dirs, etc.).

fields.name: config_path
fields.description: The config file path that was being loaded when the error occurred.

fields.name: exit_code
fields.description: Exit code, defaults to 3 (config error).

types.description: Error raised when the create_ledger() bootstrap factory fails to construct the application. Inherits from LedgerError. Carries the config path and all violations encountered during bootstrap.

types.name: VersionInfo

fields.name: version
fields.description: Semantic version string, e.g. '1.2.3'.

fields.name: python_version
fields.description: Python interpreter version, e.g. '3.12.1'.

fields.name: pydantic_version
fields.description: Pydantic library version, e.g. '2.5.0'.

types.description: Structured version information for the Ledger package. Sourced from importlib.metadata at bootstrap time.

types.name: PublicExports

fields.name: __version__
fields.description: Package version string.

fields.name: Severity
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.types.

fields.name: BackendType
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.types.

fields.name: ExportFormat
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.types.

fields.name: PlanStatus
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.types.

fields.name: ClassificationTier
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.types.

fields.name: Violation
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.types.

fields.name: LedgerError
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.types.

fields.name: RegistryProtocol
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.protocols.

fields.name: MigrationProtocol
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.protocols.

fields.name: ExportProtocol
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.protocols.

fields.name: MockProtocol
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.protocols.

fields.name: ConfigProtocol
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.protocols.

fields.name: ApiProtocol
fields.description: Re-exported from ledger.protocols.

fields.name: Ledger
fields.description: Composed application container.

fields.name: create_ledger
fields.description: Bootstrap factory function.

fields.name: get_version_info
fields.description: Version introspection function.

types.description: Documentation type enumerating the exact names exported from ledger.__init__.__all__. This is not instantiated at runtime; it documents the public facade surface.

functions.name: create_ledger
functions.description: Bootstrap factory that constructs the complete Ledger application. Lifecycle: (1) resolve config_path to absolute path, (2) load and validate ledger.yaml via config.load_config, (3) initialize .ledger/ directory via registry.init if not already initialized, (4) construct subsystem references (registry, migration, export, mock, api modules), (5) resolve __version__ from importlib.metadata, (6) return a frozen Ledger container. This is the sole entry point for constructing a fully wired Ledger application instance. No schema data is loaded during bootstrap — registry loading is deferred to first access per the lazy loading requirement.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Path to ledger.yaml config file. Resolved in order: explicit parameter, LEDGER_CONFIG env var, ./ledger.yaml.

validators.error_message: config_path must be a non-empty string.

error_cases.name: config_not_found
error_cases.condition: The resolved config_path does not point to an existing file

error_cases.name: config_parse_error
error_cases.condition: The config file exists but contains invalid YAML (not parseable by yaml.safe_load)

error_cases.name: config_validation_error
error_cases.condition: YAML is valid but does not conform to LedgerConfig schema (missing required fields, type mismatches, constraint violations)

error_cases.name: annotation_collision
error_cases.condition: A custom annotation name in ledger.yaml collides with a builtin annotation name

error_cases.name: ledger_dir_corrupted
error_cases.condition: .ledger/ directory exists but is in a partial/corrupted state (missing subdirs)

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: Insufficient filesystem permissions to read config or create .ledger/ directory

preconditions: config_path, after resolution, points to an existing readable YAML file
The ledger.yaml content is valid YAML and conforms to the LedgerConfig schema
The schemas_dir referenced in config exists and is readable (not validated eagerly — deferred to first schema access)
postconditions: Returned Ledger instance has all subsystem fields populated (non-None)
Ledger.config is a valid LedgerConfig with merged propagation table (builtins + custom annotations)
Ledger.version is a non-empty string (from importlib.metadata or '0.0.0-dev' fallback)
.ledger/ directory structure exists at the configured root (registry/, plans/, changelog.jsonl)
No schema YAML files have been loaded into memory — lazy loading is preserved
No HTTP server has been started
The propagation table is immutable (MappingProxyType)

structured_side_effects.description: Reads and parses the ledger.yaml configuration file from disk.
Creates .ledger/registry/, .ledger/plans/, and .ledger/changelog.jsonl if they do not exist (idempotent init).

functions.name: get_version_info
functions.description: Returns structured version information for the Ledger package, Python interpreter, and key dependencies. Reads from importlib.metadata (no file I/O). Pure introspection function.
postconditions: Returned VersionInfo.version is a non-empty string
Returned VersionInfo.python_version matches the running interpreter version
Returned VersionInfo.pydantic_version is the installed Pydantic version string
If the ledger package is not installed (dev mode), version falls back to '0.0.0-dev'

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function; reads from importlib.metadata in-memory cache.

functions.name: get_version
functions.description: Returns the Ledger package version string. Convenience function that reads from importlib.metadata.version('ledger'). Falls back to '0.0.0-dev' if the package is not installed (e.g. during development). This value is re-exported as __version__ in ledger.__init__.
postconditions: Returned string is non-empty
If package is installed, returned string matches the version in pyproject.toml
If package is not installed, returns '0.0.0-dev'

functions.name: validate_import_graph
functions.description: Development-time utility that validates the import direction rules are not violated. Scans the ledger package source files and checks: (1) types.py and protocols.py import nothing from ledger.* subpackages, (2) subpackages import only from ledger.types and ledger.protocols — never from sibling subpackages at module level, (3) root __init__.py imports only downward. Returns a list of violations. Intended for CI/test use, not production.

inputs.name: source_root
inputs.description: Path to the src/ledger/ directory to scan.

validators.error_message: source_root must be a non-empty string.

error_cases.name: source_root_not_found
error_cases.condition: source_root does not exist or is not a directory

preconditions: source_root points to an existing directory containing the ledger package source
postconditions: Returned list is empty if no import rule violations are found
All violations are returned, not just the first
Each violation has code 'IMPORT_VIOLATION' and path set to the offending file and import line

structured_side_effects.description: Scans .py files to analyze import statements. Read-only, no modifications.

functions.name: resolve_config_path
functions.description: Pure helper that resolves the config file path using the standard resolution order: (1) explicit path parameter if non-None, (2) LEDGER_CONFIG environment variable if set and non-empty, (3) ./ledger.yaml default. Returns an absolute resolved path string. Does not validate that the file exists.

inputs.name: explicit_path
inputs.description: Explicitly provided config file path. Empty string or None means not provided.

inputs.name: env_var_name
inputs.description: Name of the environment variable to check for config path.

postconditions: Returned string is an absolute filesystem path
If explicit_path is non-empty, returned path is the absolute form of explicit_path
If explicit_path is empty and env var is set, returned path is the absolute form of the env var value
If neither is available, returned path is the absolute form of './ledger.yaml'

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function; reads only os.environ for env var lookup.

invariants: The root __init__.py contains NO business logic — it is purely a re-export facade with an explicit __all__
types.py and protocols.py are leaf modules with zero imports from any ledger.* subpackage
Subpackages (cli, registry, migration, propagation, api, mock, config, export) import shared types only from ledger.types and ledger.protocols — never from sibling subpackages at module level
Root __init__.py imports downward only into subpackages — never circular
All shared enums (Severity, BackendType, ExportFormat, PlanStatus, ClassificationTier) are defined exactly once in ledger.types and re-exported through root
The canonical Violation model is defined exactly once in ledger.types — subpackages must not redefine it (though they may define domain-specific violation subtypes that extend it)
LedgerError is the single base exception for all domain errors, defined in ledger.types
All Protocol definitions live in ledger.protocols and are structural (typing.Protocol) — no ABC inheritance required
create_ledger() is the sole entry point for constructing a fully wired Ledger application — no other bootstrap path exists
Schema YAML content is stored verbatim throughout the entire stack — never normalized, reformatted, or reordered at any layer
All validation across every subsystem returns ALL violations found — never short-circuits on the first error
The propagation table is always data-driven (dict mapping), never code branches — custom annotations flow through without code changes
__version__ is sourced from importlib.metadata.version('ledger') with '0.0.0-dev' fallback — never hardcoded
Faker is used only for PII/FINANCIAL mock data generation; stdlib random is used for all other mock generation
Missing Arbiter integration: warn and skip canary registration — never crash
All timestamps throughout the system are UTC ISO 8601
The Ledger container returned by create_ledger() is frozen/immutable after construction

data_access.rationale: The root component orchestrates all subsystems and therefore transitively reads data across all classification tiers: schema definitions contain field metadata classified as PUBLIC, PII, FINANCIAL, AUTH, and COMPLIANCE tiers. The root reads ledger.yaml config (PUBLIC) and schema YAML files (which declare fields across all tiers) during bootstrap and delegates to subsystems that process these tiers. Direct writes are limited to PUBLIC-tier data: the .ledger/ directory structure (registry metadata, changelog entries, migration plans) which contains structural metadata about schemas but not the actual classified data values. Mock data generation (which produces synthetic PII/FINANCIAL values) is delegated to the mock subsystem and not written to disk by the root.

side_effects.rationale: Reads configuration file and registry metadata files during bootstrap to construct the Ledger application container.
Creates the .ledger/ directory structure during init (registry, plans dirs, changelog file). These contain structural metadata only, not classified field data.

fields: schema YAML field definitions with PII annotations
side_effects.rationale: Schema YAML files declare fields annotated with PII classification (e.g. email, SSN columns). The root delegates parsing to config/registry subsystems but transitively causes these reads during bootstrap and validation.

fields: schema YAML field definitions with FINANCIAL annotations
side_effects.rationale: Schema YAML files declare fields annotated with FINANCIAL classification (e.g. payment_token, account_number columns). Read during config loading and schema validation.

fields: schema YAML field definitions with AUTH annotations
side_effects.rationale: Schema YAML files declare fields annotated with AUTH classification (e.g. password_hash, api_key columns). Read during config loading and schema validation.

fields: schema YAML field definitions with COMPLIANCE annotations
side_effects.rationale: Schema YAML files declare fields annotated with COMPLIANCE classification (e.g. audit_trail, consent_record columns). Read during config loading and schema validation.

authority.rationale: The root component is the sole authority for: (1) ledger.types — canonical shared type definitions (Severity, BackendType, ExportFormat, PlanStatus, ClassificationTier, Violation, LedgerError) that all subpackages depend on; (2) ledger.protocols — structural Protocol interface definitions (RegistryProtocol, MigrationProtocol, ExportProtocol, MockProtocol, ConfigProtocol, ApiProtocol) that define subsystem boundaries; (3) ledger.bootstrap — the create_ledger() factory and Ledger container that compose all subsystems; (4) ledger.version — __version__ sourcing from importlib.metadata. No other component may define or override these canonical types or the bootstrap lifecycle.
